title: Ricinocarpus segetalis
intro: Ricinocarpus segetalis, also known as the field ricinocarp, is a perennial herb in the family Euphorbiaceae native to Australia and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ricinocarpus segetalis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Ricinocarpus segetalis is often prepared as a tincture or tea, and it is also used in topical applications for wound healing and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Ricinocarpus segetalis is classified as a plant species within the family Capparaceae. It belongs to the genus Ricinocarpus. Ricinocarpus segetalis is native to Australia. More specifically, it is classified as Ricinocarpus segetalis within the kingdom Plantae. The classification further categorizes it as a flowering plant, with its exact classification being Ricinocarpus segetalis R.Br., although this may not be universally accepted.
